title: Europa se poate proteja fără America
summary: A CATO Institute analysis argues that Europe can defend itself without the United States, countering NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte's claim that Europe is incapable of self-defense. The article highlights Europe's superior GDP, comparable military resources to Russia, and Russia's poor performance in Ukraine as evidence that European nations can deter Russian aggression independently.
talking_points: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte's claim that Europe cannot defend itself without the US is challenged as defeatist and unrealistic
The US can no longer sustain military presence in Europe due to fiscal deterioration and the rise of China
European allied nations have significantly larger GDP and economies than Russia, enabling superior military investment
Russia's modest performance in Ukraine proves it is much weaker than the Soviet Union ever was
European nations have comparable troop numbers, population, and military equipment to Russia and must act on these capabilities
author: CATO INSTITUTE
